name: cassandra.client.request.count
definition: Cumulative count of client requests by operation type (read/write/range_slice/cas_read/cas_write/view_write). This is the primary client-facing throughput metric — use it for cluster-wide rates, not table.operation.count which double-counts due to replication.
absence_means: OTel receiver not running or JMX connection failed; check collector logs
cardinality_footguns: Do NOT group by context.operation and context.service.name simultaneously at full cardinality; use top-list with top_n

name: cassandra.client.request.error.count
definition: Cumulative count of client request errors by operation type and status (Timeout/Unavailable/etc.). Non-zero rate indicates replicas are not responding within timeout. Distinguish from dropped messages (node-internal failure mode) — these are coordinator-level errors returned to clients.
absence_means: OTel receiver issue or no errors occurring (both are possible — check request count is present)
cardinality_footguns: Grouping by both operation and status simultaneously may produce many series; prefer top-list with single group-by

name: cassandra.client.request.read.latency.50p
definition: Pre-computed p50 read latency from JMX ClientRequest MBean. This is a gauge (not histogram) — use average aggregation with no post-function. Represents the median read response time seen by coordinators across all reads. No host dimension available; use context.service.name for per-node view.
absence_means: OTel receiver not running or JMX issue
cardinality_footguns: Low cardinality; safe to group by service.name for per-node view

name: cassandra.client.request.read.latency.99p
definition: Pre-computed p99 read latency from JMX. The primary SLA signal for read performance — most latency alerting is based on this metric. Spikes indicate compaction backlog, GC pauses, tombstone accumulation, or hot partitions. No host dimension; use context.service.name for per-node breakdown.
absence_means: OTel receiver not running or JMX issue
cardinality_footguns: Safe; group by service.name to isolate degraded nodes

name: cassandra.client.request.read.latency.max
definition: Pre-computed max read latency from JMX ClientRequest MBean. Gauge — use max aggregation. Max latency reveals worst-case read response times and is key for SLO violation detection and outlier identification. Pairs with p99 to distinguish sustained degradation from single-request spikes.
absence_means: OTel receiver not running or JMX issue
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality; use max aggregation to surface worst-case across service instances

name: cassandra.client.request.write.latency.50p
definition: Pre-computed p50 write latency from JMX. Gauge — use average aggregation. Writes are typically faster than reads in Cassandra (hit Memtable first); high write p50 indicates Memtable flush backlog or commit log contention. No host dimension; use context.service.name for per-node view.
absence_means: OTel receiver not running or JMX issue
cardinality_footguns: Safe; group by service.name to isolate degraded nodes

name: cassandra.client.request.write.latency.99p
definition: Pre-computed p99 write latency from JMX. High write p99 indicates Memtable flush pressure, compaction CPU competition, or a slow/down replica node. CAS write latency is included in this metric if CAS operations exist — separate with context.operation filter if needed.
absence_means: OTel receiver not running or JMX issue
cardinality_footguns: Safe; group by service.name; avoid mixing CAS and non-CAS without filter

name: cassandra.client.request.write.latency.max
definition: Pre-computed max write latency from JMX. Gauge — use max aggregation. Max write latency reveals worst-case write path stalls due to Memtable flush pressure, commit log contention, or GC pauses. Useful for pinpointing individual node write degradation.
absence_means: OTel receiver not running or JMX issue
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality; use max aggregation to surface worst-case across service instances

name: cassandra.client.request.range_slice.latency.50p
definition: Pre-computed p50 latency for range slice (scan) requests from JMX. Gauge — use average aggregation. Range scans are inherently more expensive than point reads; elevated p50 often indicates wide partitions or heavy scan workloads.
absence_means: OTel receiver issue or no range slice operations in workload
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality; group by service.name

name: cassandra.client.request.range_slice.latency.99p
definition: Pre-computed p99 latency for range slice (scan) requests from JMX. Gauge. Range scans are inherently more expensive than point reads; high range scan p99 often indicates tombstone accumulation or wide partitions being scanned.
absence_means: OTel receiver issue or no range slice operations in workload
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality; group by service.name

name: cassandra.client.request.range_slice.latency.max
definition: Pre-computed max latency for range slice (scan) requests from JMX. Gauge — use max aggregation. Max range scan latency reveals worst-case partition scan overhead and is the leading indicator of cross-partition query SLO violations.
absence_means: OTel receiver issue or no range slice operations in workload
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality; use max aggregation

name: cassandra.compaction.tasks.pending
definition: Current number of compaction tasks pending execution on this node. Gauge. Non-zero is normal; a growing trend above 10-20 indicates compaction is falling behind write rate. This is the most Cassandra-specific saturation signal with direct causal link to read latency.
absence_means: OTel receiver issue; or Cassandra node has no pending compactions (valid)
cardinality_footguns: Safe; max per service.name is the right aggregation for saturation visibility

name: cassandra.compaction.tasks.completed
definition: Cumulative count of completed compaction tasks. Counter — use rate for completions/second. A healthy compaction rate should exceed or match pending task growth rate. Low completion rate with high pending = compaction throughput insufficient for write load.
absence_means: OTel receiver issue; or no compactions have completed (unusual after startup)
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality

name: cassandra.storage.load.count
definition: [VERIFY: Tsuga reports as counter/cumulative — use rate post-function to show storage growth rate in bytes/sec. If OTel collector reports UpDownCounter as cumulative sum, the raw value may also represent current bytes on disk.] Data volume managed by this node excluding snapshot content.
absence_means: OTel receiver issue; or node has no data (unlikely in production)
cardinality_footguns: Safe; max per service.name reveals hotspot nodes

name: cassandra.storage.total_hints.count
definition: Cumulative count of hints dispatched to the hint queue since node startup. Counter — use rate for current hint dispatch rate. A zero or stable rate indicates all nodes are healthy. Rising rate indicates replica(s) are down and mutations are being queued for replay.
absence_means: OTel receiver issue; or zero hints dispatched (all nodes healthy — this is good)
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality

name: cassandra.storage.total_hints.in_progress.count
definition: [VERIFY: Tsuga reports as counter/cumulative — use rate post-function for hints-per-second dispatch rate. Semantically represents hint deliveries in progress (should go up and down); cumulative counter semantics may differ.] Rising rate means a node is down and hint replay is underway.
absence_means: OTel receiver issue; or zero hints in progress (all nodes healthy)
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality

name: cassandra.table.bloom_filter.false_ratio
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update to enable table-level JMX MBean collection.] Bloom filter false positive ratio per table. Gauge. Values near 0 are ideal (few unnecessary disk seeks). Values near 1 mean nearly every SSTable is checked unnecessarily on reads. Rising ratio indicates compaction not merging SSTables fast enough.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until table-level JMX collection is enabled in OTel collector
cardinality_footguns: High cardinality if many tables exist; always apply top_n limit

name: cassandra.table.cache.hit
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Cumulative count of cache access results per table: hit/miss/hit_out_of_range for both row cache and key cache. Counter — use rate. Row cache is disabled by default; absence is expected in most deployments.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga; also row cache is disabled by default in Cassandra
cardinality_footguns: High cardinality if grouping by keyspace+table+cache_result simultaneously; prefer filtering by cache_result

name: cassandra.table.coordinator.read
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Cumulative count of coordinator read requests handled per keyspace/table. Counter — use rate. Shows which tables are receiving the most coordinator (client-facing) read traffic. Different from table.operation.count which includes replica-local operations.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until table-level JMX collection is enabled
cardinality_footguns: High cardinality with many tables; always apply top_n

name: cassandra.table.coordinator.scan
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Cumulative count of coordinator range scan requests per keyspace/table. Counter — use rate. Range scans are more expensive than point reads. High scan rate relative to read rate indicates range query workload amplifying read amplification.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until table-level JMX collection is enabled
cardinality_footguns: High cardinality; always apply top_n

name: cassandra.table.disk.used
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Current disk bytes used per keyspace/table. Gauge. Use to identify which tables are consuming the most disk space. Disk imbalance between tables can indicate missing TTLs, tombstone accumulation, or compaction falling behind for specific tables.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until table-level JMX collection is enabled
cardinality_footguns: Very high cardinality if many tables; use top-list with top_n

name: cassandra.table.operation.count
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update. Also: double-counts at cluster level because each replica node counts its local operations.] Cumulative count of operations per table. Counter — use rate. Use only for per-table relative traffic analysis, not cluster-wide throughput.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until table-level JMX collection is enabled
cardinality_footguns: Very high cardinality if grouping operation+keyspace+table; prefer top_n on table only

name: cassandra.table.operation.latency
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Pre-computed latency percentiles (50p/99p/max) per operation type and keyspace/table. Gauge — use average aggregation + no post-function. Always filter to a specific percentile value to avoid averaging across different percentile series.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until table-level JMX collection is enabled
cardinality_footguns: Extremely high cardinality if grouping percentile+operation+keyspace+table simultaneously; always filter percentile and operation first

name: cassandra.table.tombstone_scanned
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Pre-computed percentile count of tombstones scanned per read operation per table. Gauge — use average aggregation. p99 > 100 = warning; p99 > 1000 = critical risk of query failure. Key triage signal for slow reads.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until table-level JMX collection is enabled
cardinality_footguns: High cardinality with many tables; use top-list; filter to p99 percentile

name: cassandra.table.live_sstable_count
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Current number of live SSTables per table. Gauge. Too many SSTables = more disk seeks per read = higher read latency. Compaction reduces SSTable count. Values above 10-20 per table warrant investigation.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until table-level JMX collection is enabled
cardinality_footguns: High cardinality with many tables; use top-list with top_n

name: cassandra.thread_pool.active_tasks
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update to enable thread-pool JMX MBean collection.] Current number of tasks actively executing in each thread pool. Gauge. Used alongside pending_tasks to compute saturation ratio. Active tasks at pool maximum = no capacity for new work.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until thread-pool JMX collection is enabled
cardinality_footguns: Safe; group by thread_pool and service.name

name: cassandra.thread_pool.pending_tasks
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Current number of tasks queued and waiting per thread pool. Gauge. This is the leading saturation indicator — pending tasks spike before drops occur. Monitor ReadStage and MutationStage as the most critical pools.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga until thread-pool JMX collection is enabled
cardinality_footguns: Safe; max reveals worst-case pool saturation

name: cassandra.thread_pool.dropped_tasks
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Cumulative count of tasks dropped (timed out before processing) per thread pool. Counter — use rate. ANY non-zero rate is a critical signal: the node is discarding client work. Monitor ReadStage and MutationStage drops as highest priority.
absence_means: Drops have never occurred since node startup (desired state); or metric absent from Tsuga
cardinality_footguns: Safe; rate by thread_pool reveals which operations are being dropped

name: cassandra.thread_pool.completed_tasks
definition: [ABSENT FROM TSUGA — requires collector config update.] Cumulative count of tasks successfully completed per thread pool. Counter — use rate for completions/second. Healthy completion rate alongside low pending and zero drops = thread pool operating normally.
absence_means: Metric absent from Tsuga; or no tasks completed yet
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality

name: jvm.memory.heap.used
definition: Current JVM heap memory in use by the Cassandra process. Counter/cumulative in Tsuga. Confirmed metric name: jvm.memory.heap.used (not jvm.memory.heap). Heap > 70% of jvm.memory.heap.max triggers more frequent GC; > 80% risks stop-the-world GC pauses that stall all Cassandra operations. Use context.host.name to isolate per-node heap pressure.
absence_means: JVM target not configured in OTel jmxreceiver; or Cassandra JVM not reachable
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality; group by host.name for per-node heap monitoring

name: jvm.memory.heap.max
definition: Maximum JVM heap size configured for the Cassandra process (-Xmx setting). Counter/cumulative in Tsuga. Confirmed present in Tsuga. Required to compute Heap Utilization ratio (jvm.memory.heap.used / jvm.memory.heap.max). Stable over time unless JVM is restarted.
absence_means: JVM target not configured in OTel jmxreceiver; filter returning no results
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality

name: jvm.gc.collections.count
definition: Cumulative count of JVM garbage collection events. Counter — use rate for collections/second. Frequent major GC (old generation) is more concerning than minor GC. Use context.jvm.gc.action to split minor vs major. Correlate with read/write latency spikes to confirm GC causality.
absence_means: JVM target not configured in OTel jmxreceiver
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality; group by jvm.gc.action for minor vs major GC breakdown

name: jvm.gc.collections.elapsed
definition: Cumulative time spent in JVM garbage collection in milliseconds. Counter — use rate for ms-of-GC-per-second. High GC elapsed time directly explains read/write latency spikes. GC overhead > 5% (50ms/s) is warning; > 10% (100ms/s) is critical. Use context.jvm.gc.action to split minor vs major GC time.
absence_means: JVM target not configured in OTel jmxreceiver
cardinality_footguns: Safe cardinality; group by gc.action for breakdown

name: NOT_APPLICABLE
definition: Cassandra is self-hosted open-source software. There is no per-request or per-byte cloud cost metric exported by Cassandra itself. Proxy cost metrics: use cassandra.storage.load.count for storage provisioning cost, and cassandra.client.request.count for request volume as sizing input.
absence_means: N/A
cardinality_footguns: No cost metrics available from Cassandra JMX
